changes.description: Exams with status Draft, Closed can now be deleted. Only Active and Scheduled exams are protected from deletion.
endpoints: DELETE /exams/:examId (UPDATED: allow deletion of Closed exams)

changes.summary: Support filtering archived exams on GET /exams
changes.description: The exam listing endpoint now supports an optional ?archived=<boolean> query parameter to include archived exams in the response. By default, archived exams remain excluded.

changes.summary: Support filtering only archived exams on GET /exams
changes.description: The exam listing endpoint now supports an optional ?archived_only=<boolean> query parameter to filter and retrieve only archived exams.
Introduced an endpoint to restore archived exams back to active use. Restored exams transition to Draft status (if they have no scheduled dates) or Closed status (if scheduled dates are present).

The ExamSession domain model and corresponding database table have been updated to include a face_registered_embedding field. This allows the system to store the computed high-accuracy DeepFace embedding generated during session registration for use in high-speed webcam verification during the exam.

A new endpoint allows candidates to upsert answers for multiple questions in a single HTTP request. The endpoint returns HTTP 207 Multi-Status with per-item results, so callers can distinguish which answers were saved and which failed without the failure of one item blocking the others. Each item is validated against its question type (MCQ option IDs checked against snapshot, text answer checked for non-empty string). Session-level errors (session not active, expired, unauthorized) still return a standard 4xx and affect the entire request. Maximum 100 items per request. The existing single-answer PATCH endpoint is unchanged.
endpoints: PUT /sessions/:sessionId/answers (NEW: bulk upsert up to 100 answers, returns 207)

changes.description: Administrators can now delete candidate enrollments that are in a 'Pending' status. Once a candidate has been notified or has started the exam, the enrollment cannot be deleted (must be revoked instead).

changes.summary: EnterpriseStaff can now read exam listings and details
changes.description: GET /exams and GET /exams/:examId are now accessible to EnterpriseStaff in addition to EnterpriseAdmin. All write operations (create, update, schedule, publish, close, delete, question assembly) remain restricted to EnterpriseAdmin.
The exam listing endpoint now supports an optional ?search= query parameter for case-insensitive substring filtering by exam title. Combine with existing pagination and sort parameters.

changes.summary: Enterprise staff can now view individual user details
changes.description: GET /enterprises/:enterpriseId/users/:userId is now accessible to all authenticated enterprise users, not just EnterpriseAdmin. This allows staff to look up colleague profiles within their own enterprise.
endpoints: GET /enterprises/:enterpriseId/users/:userId (UPDATED: role changed to all authenticated)

changes.description: Introduced the Proctoring Service for real-time monitoring of candidate behavior. The service supports ingestion of behavioral events (tab switching, inactivity), cheating probability scoring, and periodic biometric identity verification using DeepFace. Face verification is now available for all candidates during exam sessions.
Updated documentation to include new proctoring-related Kafka events: proctoring.event.detected, proctoring.cheating_score.updated, and proctoring.identity.verified.

changes.summary: Mandatory Face Registration for Exam Sessions
changes.description: The session start process now requires a face registration image. This image is securely uploaded to Cloudinary and stored as a reference for proctoring. The /sessions/start endpoint has been updated to accept multipart/form-data. Validation added for file size (max 5MB) and formats (JPEG, PNG, WEBP).
endpoints: POST /sessions/start (UPDATED: requires face_image file, uses multipart/form-data)

migration.before: Exam sessions could be started without face registration via a simple JSON POST.
migration.after: Exam sessions must be started with a multipart/form-data request containing the 'face_image' file (JPEG/PNG/WEBP, < 5MB).

changes.summary: Fat Event payload for Exam Grading
changes.description: Designed the exam.session.ready_for_grading Kafka event. The event is now published as a 'Fat Event' containing a unified GradingItem structure. This includes the true expected answers and evaluation criteria securely fetched from the Exam Service, paired directly with the candidate's structured answers.

changes.summary: Updated Question Schema: removed points override and metadata
changes.description: Removed the points_override field from exam questions mapping. Added an expected_answer field for short answer and essay questions, and renamed the metadata field to evaluation_criteria.
endpoints: POST /questions (UPDATED: added expectedAnswer, renamed metadata to evaluationCriteria)
PATCH /questions/:questionId (UPDATED: added expectedAnswer, renamed metadata to evaluationCriteria)

migration.before: Exam questions could be added with a pointsOverride. Questions used a metadata field.
migration.after: pointsOverride is no longer supported. The metadata field has been replaced by evaluationCriteria and expectedAnswer.

changes.description: Implemented a managed logo storage system. Enterprise logos are now handled as file uploads instead of static URL references.

The branding update endpoint no longer accepts a 'logo_url' field. Logo management has been moved to a dedicated multipart/form-data upload endpoint to support managed storage. Color updates remain supported via this endpoint.

migration.before: Logo URLs were manually provided as strings via the branding update endpoint.
migration.after: Logos must be uploaded as files via the new /logo endpoint.

changes.description: Implemented a managed media storage system for exam questions. Question media (images/videos/pdfs) are now handled as direct file uploads.

changes.summary: Decoupling of grading and score data
changes.description: The total_score and grading_status columns have been removed from the exam_submissions table. Ownership of these fields has been moved to the grading-service to ensure better separation of concerns. Related result endpoints have been removed from the candidate-service.

migration.after: Grading data is now managed exclusively by the grading-service.

changes.description: The candidate service now publishes a candidate.exam.submitted Kafka event upon successful exam submission, including both manual submissions and automatic timer expirations. This event contains candidate details and exam metadata for downstream processing.

Implemented automated email notifications for exam submissions. The notification service now consumes candidate.exam.submitted events and delivers a branded HTML confirmation email to the candidate, detailing the exam title and submission time.

Subscription plan management now handles Stripe Price lifecycle automatically. The service programmatically creates Stripe Products and Prices when a new plan is registered and deactivates them upon plan deactivation.

migration.before: System admins were required to manually create prices in Stripe and provide the ID during plan creation.
migration.after: The backend handles Stripe synchronization automatically if the ID is not provided.

changes.description: Added new endpoints to fully manage candidate lifecycle. The activate endpoint restores access for deactivated candidates. The delete endpoint performs a permanent hard deletion, cascading to all associated enrollments, sessions, answers, and submissions.
Enterprise admins can now permanently remove staff members. This operation is implemented as a soft-delete, preserving historical data for audit purposes while revoking all access.

changes.summary: Removal of cheating score from exam sessions
changes.description: The cheating_score column has been removed from the exam_sessions table. Ownership of cheating/proctoring scores has been moved to the proctoring-service to ensure better separation of concerns. This field is no longer returned in session details or list responses.

migration.before: Exam sessions included a 'cheatingScore' field populated by the candidate service.
migration.after: Cheating scores are now managed exclusively by the proctoring-service.

changes.description: Re-engineered the candidate enrollment process to use opaque invitation codes and admin-triggered notifications. Raw JWTs are no longer returned in enrollment responses or exposed in URLs. Candidates now redeem an opaque code via a secure endpoint to receive their session token in the response body.

migration.before: Raw JWTs returned immediately upon enrollment and exposed in URLs
migration.after: Opaque codes issued; JWTs returned only via POST /access/redeem

changes.description: Implemented branded HTML email notifications for candidate invitations, delivered asynchronously via Kafka when triggered by an administrator.

changes.summary: Removal of face reference URL from candidate profiles
changes.description: The face_reference_url field has been removed from the candidate_profiles table. Face ID management is now handled exclusively during active exam sessions and is no longer stored at the profile level. This change affects candidate creation, updates, and bulk CSV uploads (5th column removed).

migration.before: Storing face reference URL on candidate profiles
migration.after: Face ID managed during exam sessions only

changes.description: Exam randomization rules have been completely removed from the system. All questions must now be manually mapped to exams using the Questions Assembly endpoints. The 'RandomizationRules' field has also been removed from the Exam domain model.

migration.before: Randomization rules logic for dynamic question selection

changes.description: Implemented event-driven notifications for exam lifecycle events (Created, Scheduled, Published, Closed). The service now automatically aggregates recipient data from candidate and enterprise services to support multi-stakeholder alerts.

The user update endpoint now allows self-service updates for all authenticated enterprise users. Enterprise staff can update their own profile fields (e.g., name, phone, honorific), but cannot change their roles. Enterprise administrators retain full access to update any user and manage roles.
Enterprise administrators can now reactivate previously deactivated user accounts. This restores the user's login access and records an audit event.

Implemented a secure, token-based password reset flow. Users can request a reset link via email, which is delivered asynchronously through the notification service. The system uses SHA-256 hashing for tokens and enforces a 30-minute expiry. All responses are non-enumerating for security.

changes.summary: Password reset email template and delivery
changes.description: The notification service now consumes `enterprise.password.reset.requested` events to deliver branded HTML password reset emails to users. The email includes a secure link and expiration notice.
Implemented automated email notifications for enterprise lifecycle events including approval, suspension (administrative/payment), and soft-deletion/retention period starts.

Implemented security and lifecycle notifications for enterprise users, including account activation/deactivation, password change confirmations, and administrative password resets with temporary credential delivery.

changes.summary: Secure enterprise ID extraction for payment history
changes.description: The GET /payments/history endpoint no longer accepts enterpriseId as a query parameter. The enterprise ID is now automatically extracted from the authenticated user's JWT claims (passed via X-Enterprise-ID header by the gateway). This prevents unauthorized access to other enterprises' payment data.

changes.summary: Admin trial provisioning and refund endpoints added
changes.description: System administrators can now provision free trial subscriptions for enterprises and issue refunds for existing invoices.
Enterprise administrators and system administrators can now retrieve detailed information for a single specific payment using its ID.

changes.summary: Added notes field to Payment model
changes.description: A new `notes` field has been added to the Payment model to safely store administrative comments and refund reasons, separating them from provider-specific error messages.

changes.summary: Internal feature gate usage endpoint added
changes.description: An internal endpoint has been added to retrieve plan features for an enterprise's active subscription. This is strictly for internal microservice communication and is not proxied via the API Gateway.

changes.summary: Admin subscription plan management endpoints added
changes.description: System administrators can now fully manage subscription plans directly through the API, including creating, updating, listing, and safely deactivating plans.

changes.summary: Billing summary and invoice endpoints added
changes.description: Enterprise administrators can now retrieve a summarized dashboard of their billing aggregates (YTD paid, outstanding balance, last payment) and list all invoices.

changes.summary: Standardized pagination and dynamic sorting across list endpoints
changes.description: All list endpoints in the payment service now return a standardized `pagination.PaginatedResponse` structure. They support `page` and `limit` query parameters, as well as safe, dynamic sorting via `sort` and `sort_dir` parameters with strict field allow-lists.

All subscription management endpoints have been removed from the enterprise-service. The enterprise-service no longer owns subscription state. Migrate all calls to the equivalent endpoints on the payment-service.

migration.after: Automated via Kafka event — no client call needed

changes.description: The payment-service is now the single source of truth for all subscription state and Stripe interactions. Four self-service and one admin endpoint are available.
System administrators can now manually set subscription state (plan, status, dates) without triggering a Stripe operation. Useful for trials and manual adjustments.

changes.summary: Enterprise base model no longer contains subscription fields
changes.description: The core Enterprise object returned by GET /enterprises, GET /enterprises/:enterpriseId, and related list endpoints no longer includes subscription-related fields (subscriptionPlanId, subscriptionStatus, currentPeriodStart, currentPeriodEnd). These fields have been removed from the database and are now exclusively owned by the payment-service. If you need subscription state alongside enterprise data, use GET /enterprises/:enterpriseId/status or /summary which include a subscription_snapshot field.

The response body of GET /enterprises/:enterpriseId/status now includes an optional subscription_snapshot object. This field is populated in real-time from the payment-service. It will be null if the enterprise has no active subscription or if the payment-service is temporarily unreachable.

changes.summary: EnterpriseSummary response shape extended with subscription_snapshot
changes.description: The response body of GET /enterprises/:enterpriseId/summary now includes an optional subscription_snapshot object with the same structure as in the status endpoint.
